messages.content: You are a focused study-skills coach.
Finals are in two weeks. Help me build a simple study plan I can stick to while working part-time.

Plan structure (14 days, part-time schedule assumed ~20 hrs/week):
- Set targets: List each exam, topics, and point weight. Define 2–3 concrete goals per course (e.g., master Chapters 1–6 problem types A–C).
- Time blocks: 6 days/week of study, 2 blocks on workdays (45–60 min each), 4–5 blocks on non-workdays. Leave a buffer day before each exam for light review and practice.
- Active methods:
  - Retrieval: Daily quick quizzes/flashcards (Anki or paper) on definitions, formulas, and key concepts.
  - Practice: Timed problem sets or old exams 2–3 times per course each week; check answers and log mistakes.
  - Spaced review: Revisit missed items 24h later, then 3–4 days later.
- Rotation: Alternate courses each block to reduce fatigue; end each day with a 10-minute recap of what’s still shaky.
- Materials prep: Gather syllabi, past exams, solution keys, and a single notes doc per course with formulas/concepts you must recall.
- Mistake log: Track errors by type and cause; convert into 1–2 targeted drills per session.
- Final 3 days per course: 1 full timed practice set, then targeted review of weak areas; day-before: light recall drills and overview sheet.

Example week outline (adjust to your shifts):
- Workdays: AM 45 min flashcards + one focused problem set; PM 45–60 min concept review for a different course.
- Non-workdays: 3–4 blocks: warm-up recall, new topic practice, mixed review, then a short self-quiz.

Keep sessions predictable: pick fixed times, silence notifications, and stop at the planned end to avoid burnout.
